channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Abuja Police Take Over Probe Into Mary Habila's Death Amid Alleged Political Tensions
item.description: The police in Abuja have taken over the investigation into the death of Mary Habila, a young woman who died at the residence of Nigeria's Works Minister, Senator David Umahi. This development comes af...

item.title: Exploring Communalism Through 10 Conversations
item.description: The article presents a dialogue between a guru and a disciple, exploring the theme of communalism. The guru critiques Muslims for their perceived role in societal division, questioning their loyalty a...

item.title: Chinese Coast Guard Engages in Water Cannon Incident Near Philippine Vessel in Bajo de Masinloc
item.description: The Chinese Coast Guard (CCG) engaged in a tense standoff with a Philippine vessel in the West Philippine Sea, specifically near Bajo de Masinloc. According to reports from GMA News, the CCG vessel tr...

item.title: The 2020s: A Decade of Innovation in Cinema
item.description: The article explores how the 2020s transformed cinema through technological advancements and cultural shifts. Key innovations include YouTube's influence on filmmaking, with directors like Curry Barke...

item.title: IBM Reports Earnings and Stock Performance
item.description: IBM (NYSE: IBM) reported its quarterly earnings, revealing a mixed financial performance. The company's revenue grew by 3% year-over-year, reaching $16.2 billion, while net income increased by 5% to $...

item.title: Trump faces backlash for pushing unpopular data center initiatives despite community resistance
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump faced criticism from both supporters and opponents after advocating for communities to accept data centers, which have become a contentious topic due to environmental conce...

item.title: Activist criticizes Rahul Gandhi's protest as lacking sincerity
item.description: Gitanjali J Angmo, wife of activist Sonam Wangchuk, has condemned the flash protest led by Congress leader Rahul Gandhi near Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence as 'insincere'. In an interview wi...

item.title: Landfill Fire Continues to Burn Near Canberra; Health Advisories Issued
item.description: A landfill fire near Canberra, specifically at the Canberra Construction Recyclers site on Pialligo Avenue, remains active despite efforts by emergency services. The blaze was reported late on July 23...

item.title: Queensland records first suspected bird flu case after dead petrel found on Moreton Island
item.description: Queensland has recorded its first suspected case of H5 high pathogenicity avian influenza after a deceased petrel washed up on Moreton Island tested positive during initial screening. The sample is no...

item.title: Trump's Nuclear Deal with Saudi Arabia Sparks Controversy and Concerns Over Conflicts of Interest
item.description: The article details a controversial $63 billion development deal awarded to Trump's company by a Saudi-linked entity, raising concerns about conflicts of interest. The deal coincided with negotiations...

item.title: U.S. imposes tariffs on Canada and 59 countries under Section 301 trade investigation
item.description: The U.S. Trade Representative, Jamieson Greer, announced new tariffs under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974 against 60 economies, including Canada, following a forced labor investigation. This fol...

item.title: Idac Head Retracts Claim on Mokwele's Qualifications, Raising Credibility Concerns
item.description: Andrea Johnson, head of the Investigating Directorate Against Corruption (Idac), has retracted her claim that crime intelligence official Brig Dineo Mokwele was unqualified for an engineering-related ...

item.title: APC internal crisis unlikely to derail Tinubu's Ondo victory in 2027 election
item.description: An APC chieftain in Ondo State, Olusola Oke, has dismissed concerns that internal party conflicts over primary elections will impact President Tinubu's prospects in the 2027 presidential race. Oke, wh...

item.title: Satirical Take on Money Laundering and Political Finance
item.description: The article presents a satirical piece by John Crace, imagining Nigel Farage's right-hand man George Cottrell detailing methods to launder money while evading legal consequences. It critiques the inef...

item.title: Solar Farm in Limpopo Boosts Rural Communities' Economy and Green Energy Transition
item.description: A solar farm in Limpopo, South Africa, is showing how renewable energy projects can support rural communities while avoiding land disputes. The 347-hectare Bolobedu solar farm, built on land under cus...

item.title: PCSO Transfers Medical Vehicles to Improve Emergency Health Access in Mimaropa
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) has transferred medical vehicles to local government units (LGUs) in the Mimaropa region to enhance emergency health services. The handover ceremony, h...

item.title: The Kinks' Ray and Dave Davies Reflect on Reconciliation and Possible Reunion
item.description: Ray and Dave Davies of The Kinks have opened up about their improved relationship and potential for a future reunion, following the launch of their joint exhibition 'The Kinks: Brothers' in London. Th...

item.title: Jimothy the Raccoon's Remarkable Survival Despite Disability
item.description: Jimothy, a unique-looking raccoon in Seattle, has become an internet sensation after surviving in the wild despite having a rare cervical vertebral malformation known as short spine syndrome. This con...

item.title: Regina Police Highlight Safety Guidelines for E-Bike and E-Scooter Users
item.description: Regina police have issued a public safety advisory regarding the increasing use of electric bikes and scooters in the city. The warning emphasizes the importance of adhering to traffic laws, wearing a...

item.title: Oracle and Pentagon Enter 10-Year Software Contract
item.description: Oracle has signed a 10-year contract with the U.S. Department of Defense, valued at up to $7 billion, to provide on-premises software solutions, licenses, maintenance, and consulting services to milit...
